key_insight: STRAND turns persistence diagrams into survival data, enabling a single interpretable framework for hypothesis testing, effect sizing, and vectorised features.
engineering_relevance: This gives practitioners a statistically principled and 1-Wasserstein-stable way to compare topological summaries and feed them directly into downstream machine learning pipelines.
method: Treat each persistence value as a time-to-event and model the persistence survival function S(t)=P(p>t).
Use the shared survival representation to build a non-parametric two-sample test and compute interpretable effect sizes.
Derive a feature vector from the same representation that is stable under 1-Wasserstein perturbations.
evidence_signal: The paper reports calibrated Type I error and high power on synthetic manifolds, competitive vectorisation on 14 graph and 3 point-cloud benchmarks, and an application to fMRI functional connectivity.
watch_item: Performance and interpretability depend on the quality and quantity of observed persistence diagrams, so small-sample settings and diagram construction choices may affect results.
paper_title: From Persistence to Survival: Hypothesis Testing, Effect Sizes and Vectorisation for Topological Features
